lines.speaker: Part I Written by
lines.text: Andrew Reich & Ted Cohen

lines.speaker: Part II Written by
lines.text: Greg Malins & Scott Silveri
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is sitting in the living room and Phoebe is standing in the kitchen as the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs? Could you get that? Please?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? Just 'cause you're too lazy to get up off your touchie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! It's just that all the people in the entire world that I want to talk to are right here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiles) Okay! (Goes to answer the phone.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (under her breath) Sucker!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (answering the phone) Hello? (Listens) Hey Joey!
[Cut to Las Vegas, Joey is on the phone and wearing his gladiator costume.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Pheebs! Listen, uh can you do me a favor? I forgot the pin number to my ATM card can, can you get it for me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! Where is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, I scratched it on the ATM machine down on the corner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! So you're 5639?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's it! Thanks Pheebs!
[Cut to Monica and Rachel's]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey) Ooh, do you want to talk to Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Is that Joey?! (She nods yes) Let me talk to him!
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! (She nods no to Chandler) Because he didn't believe in my movie! Which is a big mistake because it is real! Real!

lines.speaker: A Casino Boss
lines.text: Hey! Tribbiani! Get back to work! Break time's over!
[Cut to Phoebe]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who was that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, my stunt double. Yeah, and y'know, he's getting a little too familiar for my tastes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Y'know what? I have been trying to apologize to him all week! If he's not gonna let me do it on the phone, I'm gonna go down there and do it in person.
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh Pheebs, I heard that. Can you put him on?
[Cut to Phoebe]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! (She hands the phone to Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Joey) Hey!
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't come out here!
[Cut to Chandler]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, I've supported you one hundred percent and I want to prove that to you in person!
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got that! I forgive ya! Don't come out here!
[Cut to Chandler]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forgive me? You haven't been taking my calls in a week!
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm totally over it Chandler. Friends forever! Don't come out here!

lines.speaker: A Tourist
lines.text: (To Joey) Would you mind doing a picture with us?
[Cut to Chandler]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, what was that?
[Cut to Joey]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, Entertainment Tonight. [Cut to Chandler] Yeah, okay so, good talking to ya and don't come out here. All right. (Hangs up the phone and poses for that picture.)
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is entering.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica! I'm sorry I'm late! (Starts looking around for her) Monica? (Goes into Monica's bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Phoebe? (Phoebe comes back into the living room) Oh, Phoebe, I'm so sorry. Have you been here long?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (saddened) It's okay. What the hell took you so long?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you can not tell Chandler. Okay? That I ran into Richard.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which Richard?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The Richard.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Richard Simmons?! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo! My ex-boyfriend Richard! Y'know the tall guy, moustache?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Okay, that actually makes more sense. So how was it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was, it was really nice. We started talking and I-I ended up having lunch with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so weird! I had a dream that you'd have lunch with Richard.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But again, Simmons. Go on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The strange part was, he was really nice, umm and he looks great, but I didn't feel anything at all!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! So now why can't we tell Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because it would totally freak him out and tomorrow's our anniversary. I just don't want anything to spoil that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I can't believe you guys lasted a whole year!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I owe Rachel 20 bucks!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: On a totally different bet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's almost our anniversary!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know. Can you believe it? One year ago today I was just your annoying friend Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Awww! Now you're just my annoying friend Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I got you a present!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, but it's not 'til tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, but you have to open it today! (Hands it too him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
He starts taking his time opening it. Finally Monica snaps.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (grabbing the gift from him and opening it) Okay! There you go! It's two tickets to Vegas!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: For this weekend! Oh gosh, it would be perfect, we get to see Joey plus we get to start our anniversary celebration on the plane. We can call it out plane-aversary.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do we have to?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay this is great, but Joey said he didn't want any of us out there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, he just doesn't want us to go through any trouble. Think of how excited he'll be when we go out and surprise him! Plus we get to have our own, ani-Vegas-ary! A-Nevadaversary!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I think we should see other people.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But we can go, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a great idea. (They kiss)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna go too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know Pheebs, it's kinda our (His and Monica's) anniversary.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh please, you are not gonna ditch again like you did with London.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ditch you? Phoebe, you were pregnant with the triplets!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, great story! I'm going!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering with Ross) Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, you guys, listen, this weekend we're all gonna go to Las Vegas to surprise Joey! Including me!! You wanna go?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I guess I could take a couple days off work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course you can take a couple days off work because this trip includes me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, wait a minute, wait, I've got a presentation tomorrow. I can't miss that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but I've got tickets to the Van Gogh exhibit! I've been waiting like a year for this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (coughing) Art lover!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What'd you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I said art lover.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that supposed to be an insult?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know, I'm very tired.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So Rach, maybe you and I could fly out together Saturday.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That sounds great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? All right I'll call the airlines. (Picks up the phone and does so)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Yeah, that would be nice actually, to have the apartment to myself for a night.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, so you can walk around naked.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! So I can be by myself. Y'know? Have a little alone time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Naked alone time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Phoebe just because I'm alone doesn't mean I wanna walk around naked. I mean, you live alone, you don't walk around naked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh! Why do you think it takes me so long to answer the door?
An airplane cabin, Phoebe has the aisle seat, Chandler the window, and Monica's stuck in that horrible middle seat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, so far is this trip to Vegas better or worse than the trip to London?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So it's pretty much the same Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, what about after I give you these candies? (Hands them each one from her purse.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I guess it's a little better now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah-ha! Okay, (takes out a notepad) Las Vegas 1, London 0! I'll be right back. (Gets up and heads aft.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Happy plane-aversary.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww! I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can I give you a present now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! (He grabs his carryon and starts rummaging through it.) Oh man! Don't tell me I did this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love the "I forgot the present" fake out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you feel about the, "I really did forgot the present, please forgive me" not fake out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that's okay. Don't worry about it, you can give it to me when we get back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh that's the worse thing that can happen on an anniversary ever!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sitting down) Oh good! All right, so you decided to tell him about the Richard thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What-what Richard thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no. [The patented version.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What Richard thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Monica under her breath) Simmons! Go with Simmons!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I umm, I ran into Richard yesterday and he asked me if I wanted to go for a bite and I did. The only reason I didn't tell you is because I knew you'd get mad and I didn't want to spoil our anniversary.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (talking out of the side of his mouth) I'm not mad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! Yeah, so you-you bumped into Richard! You grabbed a bite! It's no big deal. (He still ain't happy.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great!
Pause.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, London 1...

Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is coming out of the bathroom after a shower wearing only her bath robe, walks into the kitchen, and opens the fridge. As she bends over to grab a bottle of wine, her robe falls open (Damn this network primetime programming, we didn't see anything!) and she quickly closes it again. But then realizes she didn't have to do that. So she closes the fridge and stands next to the table, thinks about it for a little while and...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (takes off her robe) Oh! Look what happened! {Don't get your hopes up guys, we only get to see her from the back or from the neck up. Its times like these I wish that the networks would broadcast some nudity other than Denis Franz's butt.} (In her head.) Huh, check me out! I'm in my kitchen...naked! I'm picking up an orange. (Does so) I'm naked! (Goes into the living room) Lighting the candles, naked, and carefully. (She backs anything that might have a point like a candle on her body away.)
[Cut to Ross's apartment, he's sitting by his window looking at an art book. As he's turning the page, he glances up and notices something.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (in his head) Oh my God! That's Rachel naked! I can't look at that! I am looking at this. (Looks back at his book.) Okay, vivid colors, expressive brush strokes-Unless she wants me to be looking at that. She knows I'm home. She knows I can see her. What kind of game is she playing? I think maybe someone's lonely tonight. Oh-ho, Dr. Geller! Stop it! You're being silly! Or, am I?

[Cut back to Monica and Rachel's apartment, Rachel is singing along with a song and dancing while facing the big picture window. Y'know, I think I'd pay real good money to be on the other side of that window!]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (singing) Love to love ya baby! Ow! Love to love ya baby! Ow! (There's a knock on the door, she turns off the music, puts on her robe, and goes to answer the door.) Love to love ya, baby! (There's another knock.) Darnit! (Looks through the peephole and turns on the lights.) Ugh. (She opens the door to Ross who's leaning against the door jam.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, the scene is continued from where we left off before the break.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: May I come in?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, yeah, if you want too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you want me too?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So do I. (Slowly walks in.) Okay Rach, before anything happens (He takes off his coat) I just want to lay down a couple of ground rules. (Turns back to face her.) This is just about tonight. I don't to go through with this if it's going to raise the question of "Us." (Rachel's confused) Okay? I just want this to be (Kicks off his left shoe) about what it is! (Kicks off the other one.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And um, what-what is that Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The physical act of love. (Hisses at her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) What?! Are you crazy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh so-so you weren't trying to entice me just now with your-your nakedness?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Oh God, you saw me?! Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You weren't trying to entice me with your nakedness.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo!! No! You thought, you actually thought I wanted to have sex with you?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! (Grabs his coat) No! (Grabs a shoe.) No-no-no-no. (Grabs the other one and heads for the door.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh wow! I'm sorry, but Ross you kicked off your shoes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can we, can we just forget this ever happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes of course, absolutely! You're right. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right I guess I'm, gonna go pack. (Starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh wait! One more thing umm, do-do we still need to uh settle the question of "us?"
Ross storms off embarrassed.

Las Vegas, we have the typical glamour shots of Vegas, the Strip, slot machines, a couple other gaming tables all set to the tune of you guessed it, Money. Anyhoo, we finally get through that and watch Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe enter Caesar's Palace carrying their luggage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys wait! Guys! (Catches up to them.) This place is so much better than London! Okay? This lady dressed like Cleopatra gave me a coupon, 99 cent steak and lobster dinner. Huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you don't eat animals.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For 99 cents, I'd eat you. (Sees the casino) Okay, I can totally settle down here. It's got everything I could ever want, including Joey! Look! (Points to Joey in his gladiator suit posing for a picture with two old ladies.) Oh! Look! Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Joey! (They all head over to him, he spots them coming and panics.) Hey! Hey!! Wow! (She hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Love your condoms my man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what are you guys doing here? I thought I told you not to come.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why are you dressed as a gladiator?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, because I'm shooting a scene right now. Yeah, I uh, I play a gladiator. Uh, y'know what? Hold-hold on a second. (To no one in particular) Can we cut? Yeah, my-my friends are here, I'm gonna take a little break.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who are you talking too?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They uh director. Uhh, her. (Points to an old woman standing behind him. Who glares at him and walks off.) All right, all right, it's not a gladiator movie. I work here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?! What happened?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, the movie got shutdown because they ran out of money, so I'm working here 'til it starts up again, if it ever does.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I'm sorry I didn't tell ya. (To Chandler) I'm sorry man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no, that's okay, apparently there's a new policy where we don't have to share everything with everybody.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I knew you were not okay with that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you're a gladiator! Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, what-what's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica had lunch with Richard.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dawson?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Noo! But that would've been so cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Her boyfriend Richard!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It meant nothing! Okay? After all this time, how can you not trust me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When you go lunching with hunky moustache men and don't tell me about it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're right. I'm sorry. I should've told you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww, there we go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love Vegas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I promise you, next time I will absolutely tell you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pushing her away from another hug) Next time?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, so close.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's not gonna be a next time! You can not ever see him again!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can not see him? I mean, you can't tell me what to do!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's so funny, because I think I just did!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh y'know what? If you're gonna be acting like this all night, I really, I don't even want to be around you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine with me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine! Happy Anniversary!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa! Guys! Please! Come on! Come on! This is obviously just a big misunderstanding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it is not!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you talking...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey don't look at me! I just work here! (Walks away.)
An aircraft cabin, Ross and Rachel are on their way to Vegas.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking off her sweater) Okay umm, Ross? I'm-I'm really warm, so I'm going to be taking off my sweater. Now, I'm just letting you know that this is not an invitation to the physical act of love.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) Yep! That's hilarious!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm done. I'm done.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, last night was embarrassing for you too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, not really. I mean you've seen me naked hundreds of times.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh. But it was a first for the rest of my building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. All right, that's true! But y'know I just don't embarrass that easily.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! You totally get embarrassed!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I don't! Ross, I think I'm just a more secure person than you are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that so?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
Pause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (loudly so that everyone can hear) Hey lady! I don't care how much you want it! Okay?! I am not gonna to have sex with you in the bathroom! (Rachel sinks lower on her chair trying to hide.)
Chandler's hotel room, he's sitting there with Joey who's talking about his helmet and running his hand through that feathery thing at the top.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y'know in Roman times this was more than just a hat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure! Sure! They would uh, they would scrub the floors with it! They would use it to get the mud off their shoe. And sometimes underneath the horse would get dirty so they would stick it right...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting in the nick of time) Joey, I uh! I can't believe this is how I'm spending my anniversary.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right well, I'll take you someplace nice then. Look! A guy tipped me a hundred bucks today.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, he was playing blackjack for like an hour and he won $5,000. Can you believe that? $5,000!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, if I won $5,000 I'd join a gym, y'know build up my upper body and hit Richard from behind with a stick! (Mimics it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait a minute! Why don't I do what that guy did? I'll take this $100 and turn it into $5,000! And then I'll turn that into enough money to get my movie going again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good luck!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler! I don't need luck. I have thought this through!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I see.
Joey exits as Chandler shakes his head.
Commercial Break
The casino bar, Phoebe and Monica are sitting at the bar, while Wayne Newton's signature song Danke Schoen is playing in the background.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to the bartender) Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe this! This is like the worst night ever!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know Monica you had a minor setback in your relationship with Chandler. Big deal! It's only Chandler. (Monica turns and stares at her.) I am so sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is crazy! I mean, it's such a stupid argument. I don't even wanna see Richard again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So go fix it! Go find Chandler! He's probably up in your room! Tell him that you're sorry and that you love him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? You're right Phoebe. You're right. Thank you! (Gets up to find Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! (When Monica gets close to the door.) (Yelling) Yeah! Las Vegas, number one!
[Cut to the casino, Monica is walking through it past the craps table when she notices a chip on the floor. She picks it up and heads to the table.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anybody lose this? (Holds up the chip and the woman next to her shakes her head no.)

lines.speaker: The Croupier
lines.text: Comin' out. Place your bet. (Monica does so.) Dice are out. (The woman next to Monica rolls the dice.) Double or nothin'! Pay the front line! (Monica won and doubled the chip.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm.
An airplane cabin, Ross and Rachel are both reading as a guy stops by their row.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (To Rachel) So uh, I'm on my way back to the bathroom. (Ross giggles.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, all right. All right! Just keep walkin'! All right?
Ross keeps giggling and Rachel decides upon revenge. She gets up and kisses the rather large man in the seat in front of Ross on the back of his head. The guy turns around angrily.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! What are you... I'm sorry sir. I just, I think he just really likes you.
[Time lapse, Ross is drinking something and decides to get Rachel again.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to the guy in the window seat next to him) Hey! Y'know that teacher who had a baby with her student? (He points at Rachel.)
[Time lapse, Rachel pushes the flight attendant call button, takes Ross's drink, and spills it into his lap.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What the? What...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to the flight attendant who appears in record time. It was only seconds after Rachel pushed the call button was she there. Once again, more proof that TV isn't real, IRL she would've been waiting for the rest of the flight and by then Ross's pants would be dry.) Hi!

lines.speaker: The Flight Attendant
lines.text: Miss? May I help you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I'm sorry. Do you have any extra pants? Umm, my friend seems to have had a little accident.
Caesar's Palace Casino, Joey is approaching one of the blackjack tables on his quest to make enough money for his movie.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the dealer) Can I change a hundred? (He hands him his chip.)

lines.speaker: Blackjack Dealer
lines.text: Changing one hundred! (Gives him the change.) Good luck sir.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (betting all 100) Let's ride.

lines.speaker: Blackjack Dealer
lines.text: (Deals the cards) 13.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hit me! (He does so.) Ohh man! (Joey busts and loses all the money, but when the dealer starts to collect the cards Joey notices something.) Wait! (He holds his hand next to the dealer's hand.)
[Cut to Chandler's room, Joey is relaying to Chandler his amazing discovery.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler! You are not gonna believe this! I have found my identical hand twin!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (totally confused) What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My identical hand twin!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's an identical hand twin?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's it sound like? It's a guy with my identical hands! It was incredible! Chandler, the dealer's hands were exactly like me! It-it was like looking at my hands in a mirror!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you sure you weren't (pause) looking at your hands in a mirror?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't you see what this means?! I can forget about that stupid movie. I'm gonna be a millionaire!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (totally confused) How?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I don't have it all worked out yet, but it's gotta mean big money! Come on! Identical hands!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Again I must go back to, how?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is Vegas man! People will pay to see freaky stuff! Okay, how much would you pay to see this hand (Holds up his left hand) twice? Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Pause) Y'know, I-I can't really put a price on that Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, are you unsupporting me again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! I support you 100%! I just didn't, I didn't get it right away. Y'know now I'm caught up! Identical hand twins! It's a million-dollar idea!

Joey starts to leave to embark on his genius moneymaking scheme, but is freaked out slightly when as he goes to open the door, there's a mysterious knock. He calms himself down and opens the door to reveal Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I found my identical hand twin!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, you are so lucky! (To Chandler) Hey! So, where's Monica? Did you guys make up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But she just came up here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was Joey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I wonder where she is. That is so weird.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, she's probably talking to Richard.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Would you stop that! Do you wanna know the first thing she said when she came back from her lunch with Richard? She didn't feel anything for him. She loves you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! Now, she feels terrible! She really wants to make up! You gotta find her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (He gets up and goes to find her.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good. (After he leaves, she puts on Joey's gladiator helmet and checks herself out in the mirror) I should really start wearing hats!

Caesar's Palace Casino, Chandler is looking for Monica while Tom Jones's signature song is playing in the background (Getting the theme yet? Tom Jones, Wayne Newton, casinos... They're in Vegas people! Catch up!) It's Not Unusual, y'know, "It's not unusual to be loved by anyone! It's not unusual to have fun with anyone!   But when I see you hanging about with anyone, it's not unusual to see me cry!   I wanna die." Well, while that's playing he spots Monica playing craps and in victory hug the guy next to her. Chandler turns and walks out.

That same plane cabin, Ross is working on a crossword puzzle and Rachel is asleep against his shoulder. She shifts a little bit and Ross suddenly gets an idea. An evil idea when he looks at his pen. Then we have a little time lapse, the plane has landed and everyone is disembarking. The flight attendant is saying bye-bye to everyone.

lines.speaker: The Flight Attendant
lines.text: (to another passenger) Welcome to Las Vegas.
Rachel approaches and we see the fruits of Ross's evil plan. He has drawn a moustache and beard on Rachel. The flight attendant just ignores it.

lines.speaker: The Flight Attendant
lines.text: (To Rachel) Thank you! (Not sure of herself) Enjoy your flight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I did. Thank you very much, it was excellent. (Disembarks)

lines.speaker: The Flight Attendant
lines.text: (To Ross) Hope you had a nice flight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, it was the best!
Commercial Break
The casino, Ross and Rachel are entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think the check in is that way. (Points)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh.
A young boy sees Rachel, points, and starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello! (She makes a face and the kid laughs harder. Finally, his parents drag him off.) Ohh, kids love me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sees Ross and Rachel) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe! (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys are here! Yay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! (Hugs Phoebe)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sees Rachel's face) What? Did you go to a costume party? Let me guess umm Pancho Vila? (Points at Rachel) and you're Bob Saget. (An old lady has sat down at the slot machine Phoebe was just at.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pancho Vila?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! (Motions to her face, indicating all of Rachel's "make-up.")

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you talking about Pheebs? (Takes out her compact) I don't... (She gasps when she looks in the mirror and sees her face.) (To Ross) Oh my God, you drew on me?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you wet my pants!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa, what kind of party was this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I have been walking around like this since the plane! I can-you have so crossed a line. (Heads for the bathroom)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach! Wait! The men's room is that way. (Points in the other direction. Rachel hits him with her purse and heads for the ladies room.)
The old lady at Phoebe's machine wins. Phoebe turns around in shock.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's like the third time that lady's won on a machine I was playing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oooohhh, I'll bet she's one of those people.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: M-M-Mole people?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No-no, a lurker.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. What's a lurker?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay when you're playing a machine and it hasn't paid out, a lurker waits for you to give up and then...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Kills you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. They swoop in and steal your jackpot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How do you know about this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My nana used to do it. That's how she paid for all my dance-karate lessons.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Dance karate?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, it's a deadly but beautiful sport. (Does a karate chop, then does a little dance-type sway.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (returning with her purse covering her face) All right, it won't come off!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It won't come off!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Rach-Rach, are-are-are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, actually I took it off then I drew it back on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey-hey-hey you made it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Hey-hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who's your friend? He's hot! (Ross laughs and Rachel smacks him with her purse.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Joey) Thanks man.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. (She hugs Joey.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey listen I uh, talked to Chandler, sorry about the movie.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, don't be sorry. I don't need it anymore. I found my identical hand twin!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Your what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My identical hand twin! The person whose hands are exactly like mine! This thing is a gold mine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! That's not gonna make you any money!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Well, if that's how you feel about it, fine! None of you get to live with me in my great big hand-shaped mansion! Except uh, you Pheebs. You can live in the thumb.
The craps table, Monica is on a big roll.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right baby, come on! (Rolls the dice) Yes! Yes! I am on fire!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (walking by with his luggage) See you later Mon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait Chandler, what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does it look like? I'm going home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? Wait! Why? (He turns and heads for the door and she chases after him.) Chandler! Chandler! Wait! I'm sorry, I was just playing for one second! I was trying to find you to tell you that, look if you don't want me to see Richard again, I won't! He means nothing to me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on! I was there! (He's propped up with his hand on a statute of a naked guy. He winces and pulls his hand away.) I know he's the love of your life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not any more.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really! (They hug and kiss) All right? Let's forget about this going home stuff and celebrate our anniversary. (She picks up his suitcase.) Okay, this is empty.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I wanted to make a dramatic scene, but I hate packing.
A blackjack table, it's the same one Joey's hand twin was working at, only he's not there anymore and has been replaced by a beautiful woman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Uhh, hey. Where's the other guy?

lines.speaker: The Woman Dealer
lines.text: Which guy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He's kinda tall, dark hair, hand looks exactly like this. (Holds up his hand.) See?

lines.speaker: The Woman Dealer
lines.text: I don't know about the hands, but the guy that was here before me just went to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! (Walks away, then turns back.) How you doin'?

lines.speaker: The Woman Dealer
lines.text: Very busy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right! Okay. (Heads for the bathroom.)

Ross and Rachel's hotel room, Rachel is still trying to get the ink off and Ross is on the phone to the company. Wait a minute, why exactly are they sharing a hotel room? Didn't they like break-up or something? Did I miss a memo? Or maybe, it's just foreshadowing things to come. Who knows? Maybe the answer's at the bottom of the page. Then again, maybe it isn't and it's just one of those things TV writers just don't explain. Anyhoo...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on the phone) Yes, hello. I have a question. Umm, I used your pen to draw on my friend's face. (Listens) A beard and a moustache. (Listens and laughs) Thank you. (Rachel turns around and glares at him.) No, she didn't think so. (Listens) I know it's like (turns and sees Rachel staring at him and quickly changes the subject) anyway, umm well make-up didn't cover it and we've tried everything to get it off and nothing's worked. What-what do we do? (Listens) Yeah. (Listens) Uh-huh. (Listens) Yeah. (Listens) Oh! Okay. (Listens) Okay, thank you! (Rachel gets excited at his tone.) (Hangs up the phone) Yeah, it's not coming off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What else did he say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, he said he thought I was funny. So...(Rachel stares at him.) Okay, look-look umm, let's just go downstairs, we'll have some fun, and you will forget all about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, no! There is no way I am leaving this room looking like this!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on! Rach, it's-it's not that bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I am a human doodle!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, just because some idiot drew on your face doesn't mean you shouldn't have any fun! Okay? And besides, hey-hey-hey no one is even gonna look at you. Okay? This is Vegas! Hello! There are tons of other freaks here! (Rachel turns around and glares at him.) There are tons...of...freaks here. No other. No. Come on! No one will notice, I swear!
They both exit.
[Time lapse, they're both entering.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, there was some staring and pointing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I need a, I need a drink! (Makes a beeline for the mini-bar.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, hey y'know, they-they really overcharge you for that stuff. (Rachel glares at him.) But who cares?! Because it's all on me! (Rachel reaches into the fridge and pulls out two handfuls of those mini booze bottles.) (Watching her.) That is, one big drink!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she's finished reliving the fridge of its entire alcohol content.) Macadamia nut?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at the price list) Umm... Wow! That's-that's some pricey nut!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm! (Opens the container)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really like those Macadamia nuts, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nope! (She puts one in her mouth and spits it out, then does it again in another direction.)
The casino, Phoebe is playing on a slot machine. Suddenly the lurker sticks her head around the aisle of slot machines.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (seeing her) Get out of here you lurker! (She doesn't move) Go on! Get! (She throws a quarter at her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (arm-in-arm with Monica) Hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! You made up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I couldn't be mad at him for too long.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, she couldn't live without the Chan Love. (They start kissing.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, get a room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We have one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. Use it.
The Men's room, Joey is entering and sees his hand twin washing his hands.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh-oh, yeah! (He grabs some towels and takes them to him.) That's right, you take good care of those babies!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's me, Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Do I know you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (holds up his hand) Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Oh-ho, yeah. Yeah, the hand guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, so what are we going to do about this hand twin thing?!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Nothing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, you and I have been given a gift. Okay? We have to do something with it. Like-like, hand modeling! Huh? Or-or magic! And you know NASA's gonna wanna talk to us!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: (tries to leave) I have to get back to...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stopping him) Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! Whoa-whoa! We could have our own show! Y'know we could clap our hands together people will love it! Huh? And-and-and I wrote a song for us! (Singing, to the tune of This Land is Your Land) This hand is your hand! This hand is my hand! Oh wait, that's your hand! No wait, it's my hand!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: That's okay. (Walks out.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (following him) But you haven't even heard the chorus!
Ross and Rachel's room, Ross is drinking a beer while Rachel is examining herself in the mirror.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, I'm starting to look like my great aunt, Muriel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (gets up) All right. Y'know what? We don't have to go downstairs! We can bring Vegas up to us! (He grabs a deck of cards and pulls up a chair.) All right, come on, come on, we'll play some blackjack. Here we go. (Deals the cards.) 13.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hit me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (does so) Oohh, 23. (Rachel looks at him.) Which is what we play to at this casino! You win 10 dollars! (Holds out a ten.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I bet 20.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're right! (Gives her the twenty she won.)
The Craps Table, Monica is still on her roll, only this time Chandler's with her and she has a huge crowd of people around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shaking the dice) A new pair of shoes for the Chan-Chan man! (Rolls the dice.) Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! I've-I've never seen a roll like this in my life!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right baby! Okay, what do I want now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, ah umm, ah, a 8. Ah, a 6?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pick a number! That is your only job!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you get this one, we buy everybody here a steak dinner!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yay!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yay!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) We're not really gonna buy these people steak dinners are we?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Noo!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, good! Okay, what do I want now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh, ooh, try a hard 8.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Two fours.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (Rolls the dice)

lines.speaker: A Drunken Gambler
lines.text: (To Chandler) Don't you let her go! You're a lucky guy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you, Mister Drunken Gambler! Okay, you get this and uh, we get the biggest suite in the place! (Everyone cheers) Wait-wait-wait-wait! We (motions to Monica and him.) get the biggest suite in the place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, biggest suite in the place. Come on! (Rolls the dice.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sees the roll) Yes!! I love you! I can't even remember what we were fighting about!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's because I had lunch with Rich-Me neither! Okay, what do I want now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Another hard 8.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hard 8?! We should call it easy 8!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, I tell you what. You roll another hard eight; (pause) and we get married here tonight.

lines.speaker: Drunken Gambler
lines.text: Go! Come on! Roll!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Roll-roll!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shut up!! It just got interesting!
Commercial Break
The Craps table, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What did you just say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You roll another hard eight and we get married here tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you serious?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! I love you! I've never loved anybody as much as I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I've never loved anybody as much as I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so if an eight comes up, we take it as a sign and we do it! {Whoa! Where have I heard that before? Matthew Perry talking about signs in Las Vegas. I guess it must've been some movie I saw.} What do you say?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Come on! Let's go! All right!
She rolls the dice, but one bounces out of the table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (spots one) Okay! That's a four! And where-where's the other one?

lines.speaker: Drunken Gambler
lines.text: It went under the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nobody move! (To Chandler) Okay, you look that way; I'll look this way!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right!

He searches to his right; she searches to her left. They're both on their hands and knees when they spot the die. It's propped up against the table leg, and it's not lying flat. Both the four and the five are showing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here it is! Here it is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That could be a four or a five. It's your call.
Pause.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a four.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think so too.

Cue up the music as they move in and kiss. This time I think it's Perry Como, but I'm not sure. It's Everybody loves somebody, sometime! Everybody falls in love somehow! Something in your kiss, just told me, my sometime, is now!
The slot machines, Phoebe is still feeding quarters into the one-armed bandit as the lurker peeks over the top of the machines.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh well, lost again. (She gets up and slowly moves away. The lurker scurries in and takes her spot, only this time Phoebe set a trap for her and catches her in the act.) That's it! You and me, outside!

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: I don't want to see you lose a chunk of that pretty blond hair!
They start smacking each other's cups, but Phoebe notices a security guard approaching.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Be cool! (They both pretend to have a nice conversation as the guard walks by, but after he leaves they both start fighting again.) Okay lady, your lurking days are over!

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, from now on everyone you lurk, I'm gonna lurk first! You move on to someone else, I'm gonna be one step ahead of you, every single time! And then I'll be on your ass every hour of every day 'til Monday, because that's when I go home. When do you leave?

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: Also Monday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What time? Maybe we can share a cab!
Ross and Rachel's room, they've pretty much consumed the entire mini-bar. Needless to say, they're feeling no pain and are still playing blackjack.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (He does so.) Hit me. (She slows down with each one.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (runs out of cards) We need more cards.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and also we need more umm, drinks. Hold on a second. (Gets up but stumbles a little bit.) Whup, okay. (She makes it to the phone and picks it up, without dialing.) Hello! Vegas? Yeah, we would like some more alcohol, and y'know what else? We would like some more beers. Hello? Ohh, I forgot to dial!
They both start laughing. There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That must be our alcohol and beers! (Gets up to answer it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, it's Joey! I love Joey! (Hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I love Joey! Joey lives with a duck! (Goes and hugs Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look-look-look you guys, I need some help! Okay? Someone is going to have to convince my hand twin to cooperate!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll do it. Hey, whatever you need me to do, I'm your man. (He starts to sit down on the bed. There's one problem though, he's about two feet to the left of it. Needless to say, he misses and falls on his butt.) (Looking up at Joey.) Whoa-oh-whoa! Are you, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I'm fine! Thanks! (He starts to leave, but gets an idea and stops.) Hey Rach, how you doin'?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm doin' good, baby. How you doin'?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, don't let her drink anymore! (Exits)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, here's that Macadamia nut!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he puts it in his mouth) Nope! Something else. (Throws it back under the bed.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oops! All right, so what do you want to do now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wanna get out of the room! Y'know, I...I really miss downstairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? There's only one way I'm leaving this hotel room.

[Cut to the casino, a very drunk and doodled on Rachel is walking arm in arm with an equally drunk and doodled on Ross are walking through the casino and greeting people on their way through. Ross has some whiskers and his nose colored in, along with his name on his forehead.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well hello! I'm Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good luck to ya!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me sir, you've got a little something right here. (He points to the corner of his mouth and they both laugh.)
They've made their way to the statue of the naked man that Chandler was leaning against earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (bowing) Hello!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (bowing) Hello!

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: (bowing) Hello!
They both continue on and Ross meows like a cat.

The slot machines, we see some guy not having any luck. Both Phoebe and the Lurker are lurking him and each stick their heads around the corner at different times This is all set to that Pink Panther song. Finally the guy gives up and walks past the lurker's position, thus giving her the advantage. She scurries in and quickly drops a coin in the slot before Phoebe gets there. Phoebe arrives slightly later and pulls the arm just after the lurker deposits the coin and wins.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I won! I won! I finally won!

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: I won! That was my quarter!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! Here! Take a hike toots! (Gives back her quarter.)
The security guard approaches.

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: (to the guard) Excuse me, sir! This lady played my quarter, this is my money. (Motions to the jackpot.)

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Is that true miss?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (quietly) Sells drugs to kids.

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She sells drugs to kids. (The guard looks at the lurker.)

lines.speaker: The Lurker
lines.text: It was my quarter!

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Was it her quarter?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about we talk about this over dinner?

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: Okay lady, you're out of here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, you can't arrest me! No!! I won't go back! I won't go back to that hell hole!!

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: I'm just taking you outside!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay. (Walks out.)
The Gift Shop, Monica and Chandler are entering.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, come on, I can't get married until I get something old, something new, something borrowed, and something blue.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, all right, all right, all right! Okay! (Picks up a blue sweater.) Okay, here's something, here's something blue and new.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're so efficient. I love you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's go! (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no! We need something old!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh, great, I have condom in my wallet I've had since I was twelve.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That'll work!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, now we just need something borrowed!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks around) Here just...take this. (Hands her the sweater.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's stealing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we'll-we'll bring it back! Just put it under your dress.
She does so and it makes her look pregnant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh. (Rubs her fake stomach.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, one thing at a time. (They run out to get married.)

A blackjack table, Joey is moving in to try and get his hand twin (who's dealing) to join him in his evil plot to rule the world! "Join me, and together we'll rule the universe as father and son!" (Sorry, I had a little Star Wars creep in there-Ooh, I have a big spoiler for The Phantom Menace, Yoda lives at the end! Ha-ha, spoiled it! Now you don't have to see it!)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting down) Ahhh! (Slides his hands across the table.)

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Are you gonna play?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, I don't really have any money. Not yet, anyway... (Shakes his hands.)

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: You can't sit here if you're not gonna play.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (throws down a small wad of money, and as his hand twin starts to unfold it, Joey once again brings attention to their special gift to the world. {Y'know, looking at it now, they really don't have that similar of hands. Joey's are bigger.}) Ooh-ho-ho! (The dealer stares at him and he stops.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering and sitting down at the table.) Hello. My name is Regina Phalange. I'm a businesswoman in town on business. Would you like to see my card? (Looks down) Ooh, what did I do with my file-a-facts? I must've left it in conference room B.

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: (To Joey) 14.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hit me! (He does so.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! May I just say that you two gentlemen have the exact same hands! They're identical! Now, I've never seen anything like that in the business world.

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, Ms. Phalange, may I ask you a question as an impartial person at-at this table?

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Please stop it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wouldn't you pay good money to see these identical hands showcased in some type of a uh, entertainment venue?

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: (To Joey) If you leave now, I will chop off my hand and give it too you!
The security guard from before approaches and Phoebe tries to turn her back on him.

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Didn't I just throw you out of here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you threw out Phoebe. I'm Ms. Regina Phalange. Phalange!

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: Come on, lady! (Starts to escort her out.)

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Please, please take him too. (Motions to Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me?! Oh come on, man! You can't do this! Come on! (Being dragged out by the guard) I'm your hand twin!!
A Little White Chapel, Chandler and Monica are entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello! One marriage please!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep, we wanna get married!

lines.speaker: The Attendant
lines.text: Well, there's a service in progress. Have a seat.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Monica
lines.text: All right.
They both sit down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (singing) Dum! Dum-dum-dum! Dum! Dum! Dum! Dum-dum-dum!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's The Wedding March. Does, does that freak you out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, only because that's the graduation song.
The real Wedding March begins playing from behind the closed doors of the chapel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! (Stands up) This is it! (Claps his hands) We're gonna get married!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure you wanna do this?
Suddenly the doors burst open, and ROSS AND RACHEL COME OUT ARM-IN-ARM!!!!! And Rachel's carrying a bouquet!!! THEY GOT MARRIED!!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, hello, Mrs. Ross! (Throws some rice.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, hello, Mr. Rachel! (Throws some more rice.)
They storm out into the street.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait! (Gets her bearings) Okay!

She goes one way; he goes the other. The camera pans back to Chandler and Monica, and needless to say, they're standing there dumbstruck as The King's (Elvis Presley to the yougin's) Viva, Las Vegas begins to play. Sing along with me now, "Viva! Las Vegas! Vivaaaaaa! Vivaaaaa! Las Vegassssss!!" Fade to Black.
Ending Credits
[That's all folks, no teaser; just the big cliffhanger for season 6. Yes, there will be a season 6, and it'll start again in September. Have a good summer everyone!]
Season 5
